name: My Way Home
description_short: Nonprofit mortgage lender offering down payment assistance, affordable loans, and HUD-certified homeownership education for low-to-moderate-income families across California.
description_long:
My Way Home (formerly VCCDC) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to making homeownership accessible to low- and moderate-income households across Ventura, Kern, Los Angeles, and Santa Barbara counties in California. The organization recently rebranded from its original name, VCCDC, to better reflect its mission of supporting families on their homeownership journey.

The organization provides comprehensive mortgage lending services combined with extensive financial education and coaching. Unlike traditional banks focused on profit, My Way Home operates as a nonprofit where all earnings are reinvested into community programs. They offer affordable mortgage loan products, down payment assistance (described as "thousands of dollars"), and closing cost support. Services are complemented by free homeownership workshops, one-on-one coaching from HUD-Certified Homeownership Specialists, and a "My Way Home Navigator" tool to help borrowers identify their next steps.

What distinguishes My Way Home is its integrated approach combining lending with robust financial education and personalized coaching. They explicitly serve first-time homebuyers and those "starting fresh," with income eligibility thresholds ranging from $185,000-$259,000 depending on county. The organization hosts events like the 14th Annual Ventura County Homebuying Conference and provides both English and Spanish-language services, reflecting commitment to serving diverse communities.

The company appears well-intentioned and mission-driven, but borrowers should note that services are geographically limited to four California counties. The website emphasizes education and support but provides limited specific detail about loan terms, interest rates, or the exact amount of down payment assistance available, requiring direct contact for detailed program information.
pros: Nonprofit structure ensures earnings reinvest into community programs rather than shareholder profits
Offers thousands of dollars in down payment assistance and closing cost support
Free homeownership workshops and one-on-one coaching from HUD-Certified specialists included
Serves low-to-moderate-income households with income limits up to $259,000 depending on county
Bilingual services available (English and Spanish) reflecting community diversity
Provides financial wellness services beyond just lending, including debt management and savings support
Navigator tool helps borrowers identify their specific homeownership pathway
cons: Geographic service area limited to four California counties only (Ventura, Kern, Los Angeles, Santa Barbara)
Website lacks specific details on loan terms, interest rates, and exact down payment assistance amounts—requires direct contact
Limited information on approval timelines or loan processing procedures
No online application or loan comparison tools visible on website
best_for: First-time homebuyers in California counties served with low-to-moderate income levels
Borrowers seeking down payment assistance and affordable loan options combined with financial education
Families wanting personalized one-on-one coaching throughout the entire homeownership process
Spanish-speaking households in the service area seeking bilingual homeownership support
diagnosis: My Way Home is ideal for first-time and repeat homebuyers in its four-county California service area who qualify as low-to-moderate income and value personalized education alongside lending. The main caveat is geographic limitation to specific California counties and the need to contact directly for detailed loan terms, rates, and exact assistance amounts, as the website prioritizes mission messaging over program specifics.
services: One-on-one coaching from HUD-Certified Homeownership Specialists
My Way Home Navigator (personalized pathway assessment tool)
Financial wellness services and financial health coaching
Homebuying education covering the complete home purchase process
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 2231 Sturgis Rd Unit A, Oxnard, CA 93030
